channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Poll: Most Americans have the summer blues about Trump and the economy
item.description: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds a record low share of Americans approve of President Trump's job performance and his handling of the economy heading into the summer before a key midterm election.
item.content:encoded:
A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds a record low share of Americans approve of President Trump's job performance and his handling of the economy heading into the summer before a key midterm election.

(Image credit: Charlie Neibergall)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Here's how much the Iran war cost -- and how its effects will linger
item.description: More than three months after the U.S. and Israel launched their war on Iran, the costs and aftereffects are felt around the world.
item.content:encoded:
More than three months after the U.S. and Israel launched their war on Iran, the costs and aftereffects are felt around the world.

(Image credit: Firdous Nazir)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: With Iran deal, Trump told ships to 'start your engines.' That's not happening yet
item.description: Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz inflicted global pain during the months-long conflict with the U.S. and Israel. A tentative deal is in place, but questions remain about the key waterway.
item.content:encoded:
Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz inflicted global pain during the months-long conflict with the U.S. and Israel. A tentative deal is in place, but questions remain about the key waterway.

(Image credit: Amirhossein Khorgooei)
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman

item.title: Federal Reserve holds interest rates steady and hints at rate hike later this year
item.description: The Federal Reserve left its benchmark interest rates unchanged Wednesday, and signaled its next move could be a rate increase. It's the first rate decision under the new Fed chairman, Kevin Warsh.
item.content:encoded:
The Federal Reserve left its benchmark interest rates unchanged Wednesday, and signaled its next move could be a rate increase. It's the first rate decision under the new Fed chairman, Kevin Warsh.

(Image credit: Aaron Schwartz)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Shipping exec says industry needs assurances Strait of Hormuz will be safe
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Jakob Larsen, chief safety and security officer at BIMCO, the global shipping association, about what it might mean to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Jakob Larsen, chief safety and security officer at BIMCO, the global shipping association, about what it might mean to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Harland-Dunaway

item.title: The war with Iran is making oil changes pricier. And a deal won't solve it
item.description: The U.S. may be the world's biggest producer of crude oil, but that's not the case for motor oil. The cost of lubricants is soaring, and even a tentative deal to end the war won't solve the problem.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. may be the world's biggest producer of crude oil, but that's not the case for motor oil. The cost of lubricants is soaring, and even a tentative deal to end the war won't solve the problem.

(Image credit: Sergio Flores)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: More people are juggling work and kids -- and finding they can't give 100% to both
item.description: A new Pew survey finds many working parents feel they cannot give 100% at either work or home. Benefits like paid sick leave and more affordable childcare could help.
item.content:encoded:
A new Pew survey finds many working parents feel they cannot give 100% at either work or home. Benefits like paid sick leave and more affordable childcare could help.

(Image credit: Cliff Jette for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Is it a renter's market? It depends on where you live
item.description: About 40% of rentals on Zillow offer move-in deals, like a month of free rent, thanks to a construction boom that created an apartment surplus in some parts of the United States.
item.content:encoded:
About 40% of rentals on Zillow offer move-in deals, like a month of free rent, thanks to a construction boom that created an apartment surplus in some parts of the United States.

(Image credit: Stephan Bisaha)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Visa and OpenAI integrate Visa's secure global payment directly into ChatGPT
item.description: Would you hand an AI chatbot your credit card? This week, Visa's deal with ChatGPT maker OpenAI became the latest step in the march toward a future where AI offers to shop on your behalf.
item.content:encoded: Would you hand an AI chatbot your credit card? This week, Visa's deal with ChatGPT maker OpenAI became the latest step in the march toward a future where AI offers to shop on your behalf.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: How much of an economic boom is the 2026 FIFA World Cup for the U.S. hosting cities?
item.description: The FIFA World Cup is thought to bring an economic windfall to the cities and regions where games will be played during four weeks this summer. How true is that?
item.content:encoded: The FIFA World Cup is thought to bring an economic windfall to the cities and regions where games will be played during four weeks this summer. How true is that?
item.dc:creator: Dylan Duke
